summary: Reporter asks how to detect a corrupt/invalid JPEG image without calling `SKCodec.Pixels`, which throws an exception on bad input.

codeSnippets:
var result = SKCodec.Create(filename);
var codecPixels = codec.Pixels; // throw Exception

analysis.summary: The reporter has a corrupt JPEG and gets an `InvalidInput` exception from `SKCodec.Pixels`. The `Pixels` property intentionally throws when decoding fails, but the lower-level `GetPixels(out byte[] pixels)` method returns a `SKCodecResult` enum instead of throwing, allowing callers to inspect the result code without catching exceptions. This is working as designed; the question is answered by switching to the non-throwing overload.

codeInvestigation.finding: The `Pixels` property calls `GetPixels(out var pixels)` and throws `Exception(result.ToString())` if result is not `Success` or `IncompleteInput`. This is an intentional convenience API that throws rather than returning error codes.
`GetPixels(out byte[] pixels)` returns `SKCodecResult` enum value (e.g. `InvalidInput`, `Success`, `IncompleteInput`) without throwing. This is the correct method for error-tolerant decoding.

keySignals.text: How can I know that the picture is bad without calling the `Pixels`, since this causes all bytes of the picture to be read
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter wants non-throwing error detection for corrupt images

analysis.rationale: Issue title is `[QUESTION]` and body asks 'how can I know that the picture is bad'. The `Pixels` property throws by design; the existing `GetPixels(out byte[] pixels)` overload returns `SKCodecResult.InvalidInput` without throwing, which directly answers the question. Classified as `type/question` with `close-as-not-a-bug` because the API already provides the requested functionality.

proposals.title: Use GetPixels overload that returns SKCodecResult
proposals.description: Replace `codec.Pixels` with `codec.GetPixels(out byte[] pixels)`, then check the returned `SKCodecResult` to detect invalid input without exceptions.

resolution.recommendedReason: The `GetPixels` overload returning `SKCodecResult` is the exact non-throwing API the reporter needs and is already part of the public API.

actionability.reason: The `Pixels` property intentionally throws; `GetPixels(out byte[] pixels)` already provides the non-throwing error-inspection path the reporter wants.

actions.description: Explain the GetPixels workaround and clarify the by-design behavior
Close as not a bug — existing API already covers the request
